text.title: Selling Ideas Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding selling-ideas. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pitch MUST follow SPIN order: Situation → Problem → Implication → Need-payoff. Skipping to Need-payoff without Implication is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without Implication the audience feels the problem at low intensity; pitch lands flat.

rule.statement: Pitch MUST contain a Challenger insight ('most teams think X, but data shows Y') that teaches the audience something new.
rule.rationale: Relationship-only selling loses to insight-led selling in complex decisions; without insight you have no edge.

rule.statement: Author MUST NOT pitch the solution before the audience verbalizes the pain. Pain articulated by the audience > pain stated by the seller.
rule.rationale: Audience-stated pain is owned by the audience; seller-stated pain is theory until they agree.

rule.statement: Objections MUST be handled with LAER: Listen (full), Acknowledge, Explore (the root concern), Respond (after exploration). Immediate counter-arguments are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Immediate counter-arguments signal you weren't listening; LAER converts objection into discovery.

rule.statement: Pitch MUST end with one clear call-to-action with concrete next step + timeline. 'Let me know' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without explicit CTA the conversation ends with no commitment; one CTA is the highest-conversion close.
